title: sempervivum tectorum
intro: Sempervivum tectorum, also known as the houseleek or living roof, is a type of succulent plant that belongs to the Crassulaceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as wound healing, skin protection, and immune system support. Sempervivum tectorum can be prepared as a herbal tea, salve, or ointment to harness its medicinal properties and take advantage of its health benefits.
